text: Text similarity detection is a prerequisite for a large number of Natural Language Processing (NLP) applications. Such applications include information retrieval, document clustering, machine translation, and in particular, automated assessment of student responses to open-ended essay and short answer questions. This survey surveys existing research on text similarity detection and organizes them into three mainstream frameworks: String-based, Corpus-based, and Knowledge-based. String-based approaches focus on the lexical and spatial patterns of characters, while the other two approaches explore lexical, syntactic and semantic patterns in corpora and knowledge bases. Hybrid approaches are also emerging as combinations of different approaches are often found to improve performances and robustness of similarity assessment systems.
explanation: The passage uses concise, survey-like phrasing and includes a tautology ('This survey surveys'), which can be more common in AI-generated text. The content is generic and could plausibly be human-written as well.
